summary: Implemented the previously stubbed Great Bay stage callback fn_801F5914, added a typed Great Bay ground-vars struct, and replaced the unknown function prototype with a concrete signature using Ground, CollData, mpLib_GroundEnum, and f32 parameters. The function records selected collision/environment flag information into Ground::gv.greatbay fields and accumulates a float from arg3 / 100.0f when CollData x34_flags.b1234 is 1, 2, or 3.
what_changed: Added #include "placeholder.h" to src/melee/gr/grgreatbay.c, apparently to support PAD_STACK usage in the newly implemented function.
Replaced the placeholder comment for fn_801F5914 with a real C implementation.

fn_801F5914 now reads CollData::x34_flags.b1234 and, when the value is 1, 2, or 3, updates arg0->gv.greatbay.x10 from CollData::env_flags masked with 0x03000000, increments arg0->gv.greatbay.x14, and adds arg3 / 100.0f to arg0->gv.greatbay.x1C.
Changed the header declaration of fn_801F5914 from UNK_RET fn_801F5914(UNK_PARAMS) to a typed void function taking Ground*, s32, CollData*, s32, mpLib_GroundEnum, and f32.
Added mp/forward.h include to grgreatbay.h so mpLib_GroundEnum and related forward declarations are available.
Added struct grGreatBay_GroundVars in gr/types.h with padding through offset 0x10 and fields x10, x14, x18, and x1C.
Added grGreatBay_GroundVars greatbay to the Ground::gv union.
Included one small formatting cleanup in grGreens_GroundVars comment spacing.
smart_moves: Typed the stage-specific Ground::gv storage before using it, avoiding raw offsets for the Great Bay state updates.
Narrowed the function prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to the observed callback-style signature, improving callsite and header knowledge for future stage decompilation.
Used CollData field names x34_flags.b1234 and env_flags rather than inventing semantic names not proven by the evidence.
Preserved uncertain stage-local ground variable names as x10, x14, x18, and x1C, which is appropriate when field meaning is not yet established.
Added only the forward include needed for the newly exposed mpLib_GroundEnum type in the public Great Bay header.
decomp_lessons: When implementing a stage callback that writes through Ground::gv, first introduce a stage-specific GroundVars struct and add it to the Ground union so the function can be expressed with typed field accesses.
Replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with a concrete prototype is valuable even if some parameter names remain generic, because it documents discovered ABI information and enables later callsite cleanup.

Collision callbacks may expose useful stage behavior through CollData flag fields such as x34_flags.b1234 and env_flags; keep these accesses explicit until the underlying semantics are better understood.
For matching, a seemingly unused parameter such as arg1, arg4, or farg0 may still need to remain in the signature to match the original ABI/callback type.
PAD_STACK can be used as a matching aid when the decompiled body needs stack padding to align with the original codegen.
naming_conventions: Stage-specific ground variable structs follow the pattern gr<StageName>_GroundVars, as shown by the new grGreatBay_GroundVars alongside existing grCorneria_GroundVars and grIceMt_GroundVars.
Stage-specific Ground::gv union members use lowercase stage names, as with the added greatbay member.
Unidentified struct fields are kept as offset-like names such as x10, x14, x18, and x1C.
Unidentified function parameters remain generic arg0, arg1, arg2, arg3, arg4, and farg0.
The function kept its existing symbol name fn_801F5914 rather than being renamed to a guessed semantic name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(12), indicating stack-layout padding was needed for the match.
Stored arg2->x34_flags.b1234 in a temporary s32 temp_r0 before the conditional, likely mirroring the original compiler's temporary/register behavior.
The conditional explicitly checks temp_r0 == 1, temp_r0 == 2, or temp_r0 == 3 instead of using a range check, preserving likely source/codegen shape.
Casts env_flags to u32 before masking with 0x03000000, keeping the bit operation type explicit.
Uses arg3 / 100.0f to force floating-point accumulation into greatbay.x1C.
follow_up_queries: Find callsites or callback tables for fn_801F5914 to determine semantic names for its parameters.
Investigate what CollData::x34_flags.b1234 values 1, 2, and 3 represent in stage collision callbacks.
Determine the purpose of grGreatBay_GroundVars fields x10, x14, x18, and x1C from later Great Bay functions or runtime behavior.
Check whether arg1, arg4, and farg0 are truly unused in the original source pattern or retained only for callback ABI compatibility.
Look for analogous functions in other stage files that read env_flags & 0x03000000 and accumulate distance or timer values.
